The Princess of Wales looked typically stunning as she took part in the King's official birthday parade in London today. Kate, 44, opted for a light blue and white ensemble, teamed with a matching hat as she joined her children Prince George, 12, Princess Charlotte, 11, and Prince Louis, 8, for the annual Trooping the Colour.

Not channelling anyone, she has her own style.

Kate, 44, put on a typically elegant display in a light blue and white Catherine Walker coat dress and a matching Philip Treacy hat, and sat next to her youngest Louis, eight, when travelling in the royal carriage procession. Eagle-eyed royal fans will no doubt notice that Kate's tailored coat dress, called Lafayette, bared a striking resemblance to one sported by husband Prince William's mother multiple times throughout the 80s.

They filled it with cheap hotels, bars and eateries in the first place to attract exactly the people they now don't want.

The extensive building work is finished, the finishing touches have been applied and, just in time for the summer season, the new £6million promenade is ready - with the Mayor set to formally open it at a civic ceremony within days. The new beachfront walkway is described as the centrepiece of a five-year plan to shift the image of Magaluf from being perhaps the most downmarket holiday resort in Europe to what he now calls, rather grandiosely, 'internationally recognised as a benchmark for higher-quality tourism'. There is just one small problem in the Mayor's masterplan however: someone forgot to tell the 'sun, sea, sand, sex and Becks' crowd that they are no longer wanted. Because when the Daily Mail visited the famously brash Mallorca holiday town this week, we found that it remains teeming with young, often British tourists on very cheap package trips intent on hitting the beach, the bars and possibly even each other.

I tried to give advice to a local woman who was promoting her Cake Shed on a local Facebook group. I warned her that our authority would require planning permission (as they do for even non permanent structures and food trucks), a street trading licence, food hygiene rating registration and would lead some sort of level 2 food course. A week later she posted she was unfortunately closed as the council had warned her of a huge fine for not doing all the above. These side hustles do need some sort of legislation.

Barbie-obsessed owner Lisa-Marie Brown says she is hugely out of pocket after insurers refused to pay out following a collision involving her pink Ferrari and another car in Cardiff, in August 2023. The 45-year-old racer, who has starred in Channel 5's Rich House Poor House, claims she was left badly injured and had to stop working following the incident. However Admiral Insurance said her car did not have a valid MOT at the time of the crash and confirmed the claim would not be paid.

A Grade I listed 24-bedroom manor house in Harpenden, Hertfordshire, has come on the market for offers over £2million. EXCLUSIVEShe starred in a hilarious 1990 comedy with James Belushi before appearing on Frasier... who is this actress, now 57?

This Hollywood actress stayed busy in the 1990s with back-to-back movies and TV show appearances. Her biggest hit was in the James Belushi comedy Taking Care Of Business, which also starred Charles Grodin. The 1990 film was about a prisoner who walks out of his minimum security prison and hitchhikes to the World Series after winning tickets. When he finds a datebook full of credit cards he uses them to have fun along the way.
